workflow_instructions.mode_overview: This mode analyzes commits on the current branch that haven't been merged to main, groups them logically based on their content and purpose, generates descriptive branch names with the alexmandrik/{type}/ prefix, creates the branches, cherry-picks commits, and coordinates PR creation through the pr-creator mode with manual merge confirmations.

step.details: Ensure we're in a git repository and confirm: - Current branch name - Main branch exists (usually 'main' or 'master') - Working directory is clean or acknowledge uncommitted changes

tools.tool: execute_command - Run 'git status' to check state
execute_command - Run 'git branch' to see branches

step.details: Get all commits on current branch that are not in main: - Use 'git log main..HEAD --oneline' for commit list - Use 'git log main..HEAD --pretty=format:"%H|%at|%s|%b"' for detailed info with timestamps - Store commit hashes, timestamps (%at = Unix timestamp), subjects, and bodies - Timestamps are essential for chronological ordering of branch groups

For each commit, examine: - Commit message (subject and body) - Changed files (use 'git show --name-only {hash}') - Commit type indicators (fix:, feat:, docs:, etc.) - Functional relationships between commits

tools.tool: execute_command - Run 'git show' for each commit

phase.name: grouping_analysis
phase.description: Group commits into logical branches based on functionality and relationships

step.title: Identify commit types
step.action: Classify each commit by conventional commit type (feat, fix, docs, chore, refactor, test, style, perf)

step.title: Detect relationships
step.action: Identify commits that: - Modify the same files - Have similar commit message patterns - Reference each other in commit messages - Belong to the same logical feature or fix

step.title: Create logical groups
step.action: Organize commits into groups where each group: - Has a clear, single purpose - Contains related commits - Can stand alone as a PR - Follows a logical dependency order

step.title: Order branch groups chronologically
step.action: Sort the branch groups by the timestamp of their oldest commit: - For each branch group, identify the oldest commit (earliest timestamp) - Order branch groups so branches with older commits are processed first - This ensures dependencies are respected and maintains chronological progression - The first branch to be created should contain the oldest commit overall
step.rationale: Processing branches in chronological order (oldest first) ensures that: - Historical dependencies are maintained - The git history remains logical and clean - Earlier work is merged before later work that may depend on it

step.title: Generate branch names
step.action: For each group, create a branch name: - Format: alexmandrik/{type}/{descriptive-name} - Type: feat, fix, docs, chore, refactor, test, style, or perf - Descriptive name: kebab-case summary of the group's purpose - Example: alexmandrik/feat/user-authentication - Example: alexmandrik/fix/null-pointer-validation

phase.name: confirmation
phase.description: Present the branching plan to the user for approval

step.title: Display grouping plan
step.action: Show: - Total number of commits to organize - Number of branches to create - For each branch: * Proposed branch name * List of commits (with hashes and messages) * Brief explanation of the grouping

step.title: Request confirmation
step.action: Use ask_followup_question to confirm: - "Ready to create {n} branches and organize commits?" - Suggestions: * "Yes, proceed with the plan" * "No, let me review and adjust"

step.title: Handle modifications
step.action: If user requests changes: - Allow regrouping of commits - Allow renaming of branches - Re-present the plan for confirmation

step.title: Check if single branch plan
step.action: - If the plan contains only ONE branch: * Skip to optimized_single_branch workflow (see below) * Do not proceed with standard multi-branch workflow - If the plan contains MULTIPLE branches: * Continue with standard workflow (steps 5.2-5.6)

step.title: OPTIMIZED SINGLE BRANCH WORKFLOW
step.action:
If there is only one branch in the plan:
5.1. REBASE CURRENT BRANCH TO MAIN: - Run 'git pull origin main && git rebase main' - Handle any conflicts that occur: * Show conflict details to user * Use ask_followup_question for resolution: - "Conflicts detected during rebase. How should we proceed?" - Suggestions: - "Resolve conflicts manually (pause)" - "Abort rebase" * If user resolves manually, continue with 'git rebase --continue'
5.2. RENAME CURRENT BRANCH: - Run 'git branch -m {current-branch-name} {new-branch-name}' - Where {new-branch-name} is the planned branch name (alexmandrik/{type}/{name}) - Confirm rename success
5.3. CREATE PR VIA PR-CREATOR MODE: - Use new_task tool to create a subtask in pr-creator mode - Pass context about the branch and commits - Message: "Create a PR for branch {new-branch-name} to main" - Wait for pr-creator mode to complete and get PR URL
5.4. WAIT FOR USER TO MERGE: - Inform user: "PR created at {pr-url}. Please merge it when ready." - Use ask_followup_question: * Question: "Has the PR been merged?" * Suggestions: - "Yes, PR is merged" - "Wait, I need to review"
Skip step 5.5 - we're already on main after push

step.title: STANDARD MULTI-BRANCH WORKFLOW
step.action:
If there are MULTIPLE branches in the plan:
5.2. PREPARE BASE: - Ensure we're starting from main branch - Run 'git checkout main' - Pull latest changes: 'git pull origin main'
5.3. ITERATE THROUGH EACH BRANCH GROUP: - For each branch in the plan:
5.3.1. CREATE BRANCH: - Run 'git checkout -b {branch-name}' from main - Confirm branch creation
5.3.2. CHERRY-PICK COMMITS: - For each commit in the group (in chronological order): * Run 'git cherry-pick {commit-hash}' * Handle conflicts if they occur (inform user and get guidance) * Verify cherry-pick success
5.3.3. CREATE PR VIA PR-CREATOR MODE: - Use new_task tool to create a subtask in pr-creator mode - Pass context about the branch and commits - Message: "Create a PR for branch {branch-name} to main" - Wait for pr-creator mode to complete and get PR URL
5.3.4. WAIT FOR USER TO MERGE: - Inform user: "PR created at {pr-url}. Please merge it when ready." - Use ask_followup_question: * Question: "Has the PR been merged? Once merged, I'll update main and continue." * Suggestions: - "Yes, PR is merged - continue" - "Wait, I need to review" - Poll until user confirms merge
5.3.5. UPDATE MAIN AND CONTINUE: - Run 'git checkout main' - Run 'git pull origin main' to get the merged changes - Move to next branch group and repeat from 5.3.1

step.title: Completion
step.action: - After all branches are processed - Inform user of completion - For single branch: already on main after PR merge - For multi-branch: stay on main branch (already there from last iteration)

phase.name: cleanup
phase.description: Clean up all created branches and prune remote tracking references

step.title: Confirm cleanup
step.action: - Ask user if they want to proceed with cleanup - Use ask_followup_question: * Question: "All PRs have been merged. Should I clean up the created branches and the original feature branch?" * Suggestions: - "Yes, clean up all branches" - "No, keep the branches"

step.title: Delete all branches and prune remote references
step.action: - Collect all branches to delete: * All branches created during this workflow (from the branching plan) * The original feature branch that contained all the commits - Execute cleanup in a single command: * Run 'git branch -D {branch-1} {branch-2} {branch-3} ... {original-branch} && git fetch --prune' * This efficiently deletes all branches and prunes remote tracking references in one operation - Confirm deletion and pruning - Log which branches were deleted

step.title: Verify final state
step.action: - Run 'git branch' to show remaining branches - Confirm user is on main branch - List all branches that were deleted - Inform user of successful cleanup

scenario.name: no_unmerged_commits
scenario.condition: Git log returns no commits between main and HEAD
scenario.action: Inform user: "No unmerged commits found. Current branch is up to date with main."

scenario.name: merge_conflicts
scenario.action: - Show conflict details - Use ask_followup_question to get resolution strategy: * "Conflicts detected in {files}. How should we proceed?" * Suggestions: - "Skip this commit and continue" - "Abort this branch group" - "Let me resolve manually (pause)"

scenario.name: pr_creation_failure
scenario.condition: PR creator mode fails or returns error
scenario.action: - Log the error - Continue with branch creation but skip PR creation - Inform user they'll need to create PR manually - Continue to next branch

scenario.name: dirty_working_directory
scenario.action: - Use ask_followup_question: * "Working directory has uncommitted changes. What should we do?" * Suggestions: - "Stash changes and continue" - "Commit changes first" - "Cancel operation"

completion_criteria.criterion: All commits have been analyzed and grouped
All planned branches have been created
All commits have been cherry-picked into their respective branches
PRs have been created for all branches (or user notified of failures)
All PRs have been merged (user confirmed)
Main branch is up to date with all merged changes
All created feature branches have been deleted
The original feature branch has been deleted
Remote tracking references have been pruned (git fetch --prune)
User is informed of final state and any manual actions needed

important_notes.note: Always work from main branch when creating new branches
Use chronological order when cherry-picking commits within a group
Use new_task tool to delegate PR creation to pr-creator mode
Wait for user confirmation after each PR is created before continuing
Pull latest main after each merge to keep base current
Preserve commit hashes and messages accurately
Handle conflicts gracefully with user input
